signal_title: Protecting Americans' Data from Foreign Adversaries Act of 2024
risk_rationale: The new legislation imposes restrictions on data sales to foreign adversaries, which could impact TikTok's data handling practices, particularly concerning cross-border data transfers and data sovereignty policies.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Government access transparency with annual transparency report
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Restrictions on data sales to foreign adversaries
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not explicitly address restrictions on data sales to foreign adversaries, which is a new requirement under the legislation.

remediations.title: Implement Data Sale Restrictions
remediations.description: Develop and enforce policies to ensure no U.S. user data is sold to entities classified as foreign adversaries. This includes updating data handling protocols and enhancing transparency measures.
acceptance_criteria: No data sales to foreign adversaries
Updated transparency reports reflecting compliance with the new legislation

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new legislation could result in significant legal and financial penalties, as well as damage to TikTok's reputation in the U.S. market.
recommended_actions: Review and update data handling policies to comply with the new legislation
Enhance transparency measures to include compliance with restrictions on data sales
Coordinate with legal and policy teams to ensure alignment with national security safeguards
